ut2-5-1.title: Hands-on: capillary flow — how adhesive fillets form
ut2-5-1.intro: Adhesive fillets climb honeycomb cell walls by the same capillary action that pulls penetrant into this magnified crack. Fill the crack, then race a thin grade against a thick one to see how viscosity decides whether a liquid wets a tight gap — or bridges it and leaves a void.

tasks.label: Apply penetrant and let capillarity fill the gap
tasks.hint: Apply and wait — fill depth grows as the square root of time (Washburn), the same wetting that builds core fillets during cure.

tasks.label: Fill at low and high viscosity; compare the curves
tasks.hint: Run one fill at each end of the viscosity slider — the depth-time chart overlays both runs, like runny paste adhesive versus stiff film.

ut2-5-2.title: Hands-on: read disbond depth from echo arrival
ut2-5-2.intro: A disbond turns its interface into an air-backed reflector, and pulse-echo's advantage is depth: echo arrival time tells you which bondline failed. Treat each step of this aluminum block as a deeper failed interface in a skin-doubler stack — gauge three, then flag the shallowest as the near-side disbond.

tasks.label: Gauge the reflector depth on three different steps
tasks.hint: Seat the 0-degree probe mid-step and read the backwall echo — an earlier arrival means sound stopped at a shallower interface.

tasks.label: Flag the shallowest step — the near-side disbond
tasks.hint: A near-side disbond stops sound at the first bondline, so its echo arrives earliest; a far-side break lets sound travel deeper first.

ut2-5-3.title: Hands-on: standardize on a reference — null, then deviate
ut2-5-3.intro: A bond evaluation is only valid after standardizing on a known-good reference: the tester is nulled there, and every finding is read as a deviation from that null. Practice the discipline — null the probe at contact, then lift off and watch the dot swing away exactly as it would over a disbonded, un-loaded skin.

tasks.label: Lower to contact and null on the known-good surface
tasks.hint: Get the probe within 0.15 mm of the surface before pressing Null — standardization demands real contact with the reference.

tasks.label: Lift off and watch the dot swing away from the null
tasks.hint: Raise the probe about 2 mm after a good null; a probe the structure no longer loads drifts toward AIR, like a meter over a disbond.
